what_it_does: Declares a generated surface specification for view C1 in area C, mapping the journeys it belongs to, the addresses it surfaces, and the components and build targets it feeds.
observations: It is a surface spec for view C1 under the journey-spine directory J3, representing INB-lanes and EVT-now.
Status is planned and the surface targets desktop and mobile platforms.
Home journey is J3; journeys also include J5 and J7, with cross_journeys explicitly listing J5 and J7.
Addresses cover ui://activity and sub-paths, ui://inbox, ui://inspector/inbox, and ui://settings/inbox.
build_to names components activity, inbox, inspector, and settings, plus a design lint command and a tokens-to-CSS pipeline.
The _what preamble states the file was generated by a Claude Code session and instructs never to invent an address.
standouts: Self-documenting provenance and an explicit address-authority rule in _what.
Separation of home_journey (J3) from cross_journeys (J5, J7).
build_to bundles components, a lint command, and token source into one object.
conventions: Follows a structured surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, journeys, addresses, and build_to.
Uses ui:// URI-style identifiers for addressable surfaces.
Includes a human-readable _what field explaining file semantics and provenance.
Separates home journey from the full journey list and cross-journey references.
concerns: build_to.design_lint mixes a shell command with a parenthetical gate label, making it documentation rather than a clean machine target.
build_to.tokens is a sentence with an arrow and note, not a parseable reference.
represents values (INB-lanes, EVT-now) are abbreviations with no in-file expansion.
No symbols array is present; the file notes this explicitly.
notes: A planned, cross-journey surface spec for view C1 that wires four components to a set of ui:// addresses and records its own generation rules.
questions: What do INB-lanes and EVT-now specifically represent?
What is the B3 web mentioned in _what?
Are cross_journeys always a subset of journeys, and what semantics distinguish them?
What is the exact mapping between each ui:// address and the listed components?
Is design_lint intended as an executable build step or as documentation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to specify a generated surface for view C1 with journey/address wiring and build targets; the file matches that purpose, although build_to values are partly human-readable rather than strictly machine-actionable.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to establish a single, authoritative surface definition that links a UI view to its journeys, addressable UI surfaces, components, lint gates, and token sources, while recording how it was produced.
novelty: The _what field combines schema contract, provenance, and an address-authority constraint, and build_to mixes components, lint, and token pipeline in one target object.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, self-described surface specification with journey membership, address coverage, and downstream build targets that a fused ledger could use to trace which views, journeys, components, and addresses belong together.
summary_for_embedding: A planned JSON surface specification for view C1 in area C, home journey J3, spanning desktop and mobile, representing INB-lanes and EVT-now, listing journeys J3/J5/J7, ui:// addresses, and build targets for activity, inbox, inspector, settings, design lint, and tokens.
intention_for_embedding: To record the generated design surface for view C1 and map it to its journeys, addressable UI surfaces, components, and build pipeline.
